title: Rivian Q1 2026 Earnings: Top Estimates but Investor Caution
text: Rivian topped Q1 2026 earnings estimates on revenue but faced persistent investor caution about the company's path to profitability. The company beat analyst revenue expectations, reflecting strong R2 pre-launch demand and regulatory credit sales. Despite the revenue beat, investors remained concerned about cash burn and execution risks. Net cash used in operating activities hit $703 million in Q1 2026, while free cash flow was negative $1.075 billion. The company ended the quarter with $4.83 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments after receiving a $1 billion Volkswagen investment. Rivian maintains a healthy liquidity cushion of $6.1 billion. The R2 launch is proceeding on schedule with customer deliveries expected by June 2026. Production ramp expectations are critical to investor confidence. The company faces near-term cash burn challenges but has mitigated longer-term funding risks through the Volkswagen partnership and DOE loan. Analyst sentiment remains mixed with concerns about demand sustainability and competitive pressures.
